CASE MANAGEMENT STATEMENT (TRANSACTION ID # 60256212) FILED BY DEFENDANT AARON GORDON CONSTRUCTION, INC. JURY DEMANDED, ESTIMATED TIME FOR TRIAL: 5.0 DAYS

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT AARON GORDON CONSTRUCTION, INC.'S MOTION TO STRIKE PORTIONS OF THE FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT FILED BY PLAINTIFF MARIA AMBRIZ DE VELASQUEZ. THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING: GRANTED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THE FIRST TWO PARAGRAPHS OF THE FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT AND THE TWO REFERENCES TO PUNITIVE DAMAGES ALLEGATIONS AND DENIED AS TO THE REFERENCES TO COMPENSATORY DAMAGES AND MEDICAL BILLS. THE FIRST TWO PARAGRAPHS OF THE FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT APPEAR TO BE A REPLY TO DEFENDANT WESTCO ROOFING CO.'S ANSWER WHICH IS NOT APPROPRIATE TO INCLUDE IN A COMPLAINT. THE REFERENCES TO PUNITIVE DAMAGES SHOULD BE STRICKEN BECAUSE THERE ARE NO ALLEGATIONS OF ULTIMATE FACTS SHOWING ANY MALICE, OPPRESSION OR FRAUD ON THE PART OF ANY OF THE DEFENDANTS. THE REFERENCES TO COMPENSATORY DAMAGES AND MEDICAL BILLS ARE APPROPRIATE TO INCLUDE IN A COMPLAINT. PREVAILING PARTY SHALL PREPARE FORM OF ORDER THAT COMPLIES WITH CRC 3.1312. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; CLERK: FELICIA GREEN; COURT REPORTER: CAROL KAREN, CSR NO. 8189
SUBSTITUTION OF ATTORNEY, (TRANSACTION ID # 60596743): KOZLOW, ANDREW J. SUBSTITUTED FOR VUCINICH, JEFFERY MICHAEL AS ATTORNEY FOR AARON GORDON CONSTRUCTION, INC.

calendar.matters:
MASTER CALENDAR COURT
Off Calendar Nov-20-2017 Vacated

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Apr-19-2017 Set For Trial On Dec-18-17 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Mar-03-2017 Continued To May-03-17 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Defendant Aaron Gordon Construction, Inc.S Notice Of Motion And Motion To Strike Portions Of The First Amended Complaint
Off Calendar Mar-17-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Matthew E. Lee, of Clapp Moroney Vucinich Beeman Scheley, Tel. 650.989.5400, for defendants;
No other appearances.

Ruling: The matter was not argued. The Court adopts its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT AARON GORDON CONSTRUCTION, INC.'s Motion To Strike Portions Of The First Amended Complaint filed by plaintiff Maria Ambriz de Velasquez is granted without leave to amend as to the first two paragraphs of the first amended complaint and the two references to punitive damages allegations and denied as to the references to compensatory damages and medical bills. The first two paragraphs of the first amended complaint appear to be a reply to defendant Westco Roofing Co.'s answer which is not appropriate to include in a complaint. The references to punitive damages should be stricken because there are no allegations of ultimate facts showing any malice, oppression or fraud on the part of any of the defendants. The references to compensatory damages and medical bills are appropriate to include in a complaint. Prevailing party shall prepare form of order that complies with CRC 3.1312. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Felicia Green; Court Reporter: Carol Karen, CSR No. 8189, email: ckaren@sftc.org; Dept. 302.

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jan-13-2017 Continued To Mar-22-17 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Aug-12-2016 Continued To Oct-12-16 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Leave To Filed A First Amended Complaint
Off Calendar Aug-23-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances.

The Court confirms the tentative ruling.

PLAINTIFF MARIA AMBRIZ DE VELASQUEZ'S Motion For Leave To Filed A First Amended Complaint is GRANTED. The first amended complaint - by a pro per plaintiff - adds a defendant and provides adequate notice of her claims. Prevailing party to submit a [proposed] written order in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Richard Ulmer; Clerk: Jose Rios Merida; Reporter: Reyes Hunter, CSR #6576, bus. tel: (530) 339-5023, email: rhunter@sftc.org. (D302)
